text:
Once upon a time in a quiet town nestled among rolling countryside, a peculiar squirrel named Oliver lived in a cozy oak tree. Oliver was no ordinary squirrel; he dreamed of traveling the world, visiting museums and studying ancient artifacts.

One sunny morning, Oliver climbed down from his tree onto what looked like a bridge connecting two fields. He hopped off deliberately, searching for something different—a feather he’d once seen an old collector mention. That night, while curled up in his home under a map of continents, Oliver imagined himself crossing those fields on a tour through Europe.

The next day, Oliver ventured into the town. His eyes danced with excitement as he spotted the local museum. He tucked himself in a corner filled with statues. The exhibit on Egyptian art intrigued him; he fancied a pectoral budjet. After some time, he felt peckish. He scampered over to the bake house, spotting some freshly made lemon bars.
